title: Ovary
body_html:
Arterial blood supply
  • Ovarian arteries are direct branches of the abdominal aorta

Lymph drainage
  • Para-aortic pattern.
  • Additional potential for palpable inguinal lymphadenopathy in disease

Innervation
  • From the ovarian and pelvic plexuses (transmitted within suspensory ligament)
  • Visceral afferent pain fibres ascend with sympathetic fibres to T10-L1 (mainly T10-T11) spinal segments hence ovarian pain may radiate to the umbilicus*
  • Parasympathetic supply from S2-S4 fibres originating from abdominal autonomic plexuses.

*Pain may radiate to the medial thigh due to irritation of the obturator nerve as it crosses the ovarian fossa



Image sourced from Wikipedia
Diagram illustrating the arterial blood supply to the female reproductive tract

Overview of the anatomy of the female reproductive tract
